summary: Iteration 32 — DEMOSOFU/BETADOLLAR coupon validation, Admin Orders shape/auth, and Tricky Testaments wrong-answer deduction. Backend pytest 14/14 PASSED. Tricky Testaments wrong-answer deduction VERIFIED in browser (label 'Missed it -100', score 0 -> -100). Admin Orders BLOCKED by frontend safeJson misuse — API returns 77 orders correctly but UI renders 'Orders (0) / No orders found' because AdminOrders.fetchOrders treats safeJson() return value as the API body.

integration_issues.issue: AdminOrders.js uses 'const data = await safeJson(res, {})' then reads data.items / data.orders. But safeJson(response) returns { ok, status, data }, so data.items is always undefined and orders state stays []. Network call is correct (200 with 77 items confirmed via in-page eval), only the destructuring is wrong. Same bug in fetchRefundRequests (line 78 reads data.requests on the wrapper, not the body).

action_items: FIX AdminOrders.js fetchOrders (line 56-71): change 'const data = await safeJson(res, {});' to 'const { data } = await safeJson(res);' so that data.items resolves to the API body's items array.
FIX AdminOrders.js fetchRefundRequests (line 73-84): same destructuring fix — 'const { data } = await safeJson(res);' before reading data.requests.
Optional: Audit other safeJson callsites in AdminOrders.js (lines 131, 151, 170, 192 already use the correct '{ ok, data }' destructure) to confirm consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: safeJson returns { ok, status, data } — must always be destructured. Two callsites in AdminOrders.js break this contract (lines 62, 78). The other four callsites in the same file get it right; this is an isolated copy/paste regression introduced when fetchOrders was rewritten from XHR to fetch.

Backend coupon_routes._validate_redemption_code_as_coupon correctly: (1) treats demo+test code_type only, (2) auto-expires past expires_at on read, (3) blocks REVOKED/EXPIRED, (4) enforces uses_used >= max_uses, (5) restricts demo codes to holiday/breakfast/bkft/hol prefixes, (6) returns override_total=1.0 for test codes. All 10 backend cases pass.
/api/coupons/use/{code} now correctly falls back to redemption_codes.uses_used $inc when not in coupons collection — verified via mongo direct query.

Only one /api/admin/orders GET route registered (routes/admin_routes.py line 952); duplicate at line 2060+ confirmed deleted. Resend-email route at line 1053 only; /send-email alias gone (returns 404 as expected).
TrickyTestamentGame.js Missed it! button now deducts both DD-wager and regular point value; label correctly shows 'Missed it -<n>'. Verified live: 0 -> -100 Stacks on a 100-pt regular tile.
Preview /api/admin/content-health returns broken_link_count=0 — confirms content tree is intact in preview (production /app/content/ deploy gap is out of scope as noted).

success_rate.frontend: 50% (Tricky Testaments PASS; Admin Orders blocked by safeJson destructure bug)

test_credentials: Admin: overflowharvest@gmail.com / Admin123! — used 'Continue without 2FA' on /2fa-setup
seed_data_creation: DEMOSOFU79.uses_used was incremented to 1 then to 5 during max-uses test, status briefly REVOKED — autouse fixture restores it to uses_used=0, status=ACTIVE before AND after each test. Final state: DEMOSOFU79 uses_used=0 ACTIVE. All other codes untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend coupon validation for DEMOSOFU/BETADOLLAR fully verified (14/14 pytest pass). Tricky Testaments deduction verified live. ONLY remaining item: AdminOrders.js safeJson destructure bug — once main agent applies the 2-line fix, retest /admin/orders UI in browser to confirm the 77 orders render and refund-requests alert banner shows up. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_32_coupons_admin_orders.py is reusable for regression. Per request, Phase 2 user-side redemption flow + production fulfillment files (/app/content/ deploy gap) remain out of scope.
rca of the issue: Admin Orders 'zero rows' RCA — Reproduction: log in as admin, go to /admin/orders, observe 'Orders (0) / No orders found' even though backend has 77 orders. Verified via in-browser fetch eval: GET /api/admin/orders?limit=100 returns 200 with {items:[77 items], total:77, ...}. Root cause: safeJson(response) returns wrapper {ok,status,data}, but fetchOrders does 'const data = await safeJson(res, {})' and then reads data.items — which doesn't exist on the wrapper. Always returns []. Mitigation: change to 'const { data } = await safeJson(res);' (matches the pattern already used on lines 131/151/170/192 of the same file). Same one-line fix needed for fetchRefundRequests.
